    for t in K:-1:1
        # Optimize estimating equation for time t
        result = optimize(psi -> estimating_equation(psi, t), psi_hat, BFGS())
        psi_hat = result.minimizer
    end
    
    return psi_hat
end

# Apply G-estimation to the simulated data
psi_estimates = g_estimation(data, treatment_vars, covariate_vars, "Y")
println("G-estimation results:")
println("ψ₀ (baseline effect): ", psi_estimates[1])
println("ψ₁ (L interaction): ", psi_estimates[2]) 
println("ψ₂ (history interaction): ", psi_estimates[3])

6.5 The G-Computation Formula

6.5.1 Theoretical Development

The g-computation formula, also known as the parametric g-formula, provides a direct approach to causal inference by modeling the full data-generating process. This method simulates what would happen under different treatment strategies by iteratively applying estimated models for the time-varying confounders and outcomes.

Definition 10.5 (G-Computation Formula). Under sequential exchangeability and positivity, the mean potential outcome under treatment strategy ā is:

E[Y(ā)] = ∫ E[Y | L̄_K, Ā_K = ā] ∏{t=0}^K f(L_t | L̄{t-1}, Ā_{t-1}) dL̄_K

where f(L_t | L̄{t-1}, Ā{t-1}) is the conditional density of confounders at time t.

6.5.2 Parametric Implementation

The parametric g-formula proceeds by:

  1. Fitting regression models for each time-varying confounder L_t given past history
  2. Fitting a model for the outcome Y given full covariate and treatment history
  3. Monte Carlo simulation to compute E[Y(ā)] for any treatment strategy ā
using Distributions, Random

function parametric_g_formula(data::DataFrame, treatment_vars::Vector{String},
                            covariate_vars::Vector{String}, outcome_var::String,
                            treatment_strategy::Function, n_sim::Int = 10000)
    """
    Implement the parametric g-formula via Monte Carlo simulation
    """
    K = length(treatment_vars)
    
    # Fit models for time-varying confounders
    confounder_models = Dict()
    for t in 2:length(covariate_vars)  # Skip baseline L0
        y_var = covariate_vars[t]
        x_vars = vcat(covariate_vars[1:(t-1)], treatment_vars[1:(t-1)])
        
        # Create formula dynamically
        formula_str = "$y_var ~ " * join(x_vars, " + ")
        formula = eval(Meta.parse("@formula($formula_str)"))
        
        model = lm(formula, data)
        confounder_models[t] = model
    end
    
    # Fit outcome model
    outcome_formula_str = "$outcome_var ~ " * 
                         join(vcat(covariate_vars, treatment_vars), " + ")
    outcome_formula = eval(Meta.parse("@formula($outcome_formula_str)"))
    outcome_model = lm(outcome_formula, data)
    
    # Monte Carlo simulation
    Random.seed!(42)
    simulated_outcomes = Float64[]
    
    for sim in 1:n_sim
        # Sample individual from observed data
        i = rand(1:nrow(data))
        
        # Initialize with observed baseline covariates
        sim_data = Dict()
        sim_data[covariate_vars[1]] = data[i, covariate_vars[1]]
        
        # Forward simulation
        for t in 1:K
            # Apply treatment strategy
            history = Dict(var => get(sim_data, var, 0.0) for var in keys(sim_data))
            sim_data[treatment_vars[t]] = treatment_strategy(history, t)
            
            # Simulate next time-varying confounder if exists
            if t < length(covariate_vars)
                next_cov_idx = t + 1
                if next_cov_idx <= length(covariate_vars)
                    # Predict mean of next confounder
                    pred_vars = vcat(covariate_vars[1:next_cov_idx-1], 
                                   treatment_vars[1:t])
                    
                    X_pred = [get(sim_data, var, 0.0) for var in pred_vars]
                    model = confounder_models[next_cov_idx]
                    
                    # Create DataFrame for prediction
                    pred_df = DataFrame()
                    for (j, var) in enumerate(pred_vars)
                        pred_df[!, var] = [X_pred[j]]
                    end
                    
                    μ = predict(model, pred_df)[1]
                    σ = sqrt(deviance(model) / dof_residual(model))
                    
                    sim_data[covariate_vars[next_cov_idx]] = rand(Normal(μ, σ))
                end
            end
        end
        
        # Predict final outcome
        outcome_vars = vcat(covariate_vars, treatment_vars)
        X_outcome = [get(sim_data, var, 0.0) for var in outcome_vars]
        
        outcome_df = DataFrame()
        for (j, var) in enumerate(outcome_vars)
            outcome_df[!, var] = [X_outcome[j]]
        end
        
        y_pred = predict(outcome_model, outcome_df)[1]
        push!(simulated_outcomes, y_pred)
    end
    
    return mean(simulated_outcomes), std(simulated_outcomes) / sqrt(n_sim)
end

# Define treatment strategies
always_treat(history, t) = 1.0
never_treat(history, t) = 0.0

function threshold_strategy(history, t)
    # Treat if most recent confounder exceeds threshold
    L_current = get(history, "L$(t-1)", 0.0)  
    return L_current > 0.5 ? 1.0 : 0.0
end

# Compare strategies using g-computation
println("G-computation results:")

result_always = parametric_g_formula(data, treatment_vars, covariate_vars, 
                                   "Y", always_treat, 5000)
println("Always treat: ", round(result_always[1], digits=3), " ± ", 
        round(result_always[2], digits=3))

result_never = parametric_g_formula(data, treatment_vars, covariate_vars,
                                  "Y", never_treat, 5000)  
println("Never treat: ", round(result_never[1], digits=3), " ± ",
        round(result_never[2], digits=3))

result_threshold = parametric_g_formula(data, treatment_vars, covariate_vars,
                                      "Y", threshold_strategy, 5000)
println("Threshold strategy: ", round(result_threshold[1], digits=3), " ± ",
        round(result_threshold[2], digits=3))

println("Treatment effect (always vs never): ", 
        round(result_always[1] - result_never[1], digits=3))

6.6 Target Trial Emulation

6.6.1 Conceptual Framework

Target trial emulation addresses the challenge of drawing causal inferences from observational data by explicitly defining the randomized controlled trial (RCT) that would ideally answer the research question. This approach helps clarify the estimand, identify potential biases, and guide analytical choices.

Definition 10.6 (Target Trial). A target trial is a hypothetical randomized experiment that would provide an ideal answer to the causal question of interest, specified by: - Eligibility criteria - Treatment strategies
- Assignment procedures - Follow-up period - Outcome definition - Causal contrasts of interest - Analysis plan

6.6.2 Protocol Development

Consider estimating the effect of immediate versus delayed ART initiation in HIV patients. The target trial protocol might specify:

Eligibility: HIV-positive patients with CD4 count between 350-500 cells/μL Treatment strategies: - Strategy 1: Initiate ART immediately
- Strategy 2: Defer ART until CD4 < 350 or AIDS diagnosis Assignment: Random 1:1 allocation at baseline Follow-up: 5 years from randomization Outcome: All-cause mortality Analysis: Intention-to-treat comparison using Kaplan-Meier survival curves

6.6.3 Emulation Using Observational Data

Emulating this trial from observational data requires:

  1. Cohort construction: Identify patients meeting eligibility criteria at a baseline time (“time zero”)
  2. Strategy assignment: Classify observed treatment patterns as adherent to one strategy
  3. Outcome ascertainment: Apply identical outcome definitions and follow-up periods
  4. Bias adjustment: Account for confounding, selection bias, and measurement issues

```julia using Survival, DataFrames, Dates

function emulate_target_trial(data::DataFrame, eligibility_criteria::Function, strategy_classifier::Function, followup_years::Int = 5) ““” Emulate target trial from longitudinal observational data ““”

# Step 1: Apply eligibility criteria and define time zero
eligible_data = DataFrame()

for id in unique(data.patient_id)
    patient_data = filter(row -> row.patient_id == id, data)
    sort!(patient_data, :visit_date)
    
    # Find first visit meeting eligibility criteria
    for (i, row) in enumerate(eachrow(patient_data))
        if eligibility_criteria(row)
            # Define this as time zero for this patient
            baseline = copy(row)
            baseline.time_zero = row.visit_date
            baseline.baseline_cd4 = row.cd4_count
            baseline.baseline_age = row.age
            
            # Extract subsequent visits for strategy classification
            future_visits = patient_data[(i+1):end, :]
            baseline.strategy = strategy_classifier(baseline, future_visits)
            
            push!(eligible_data, baseline)
            break  # Only use first eligible visit per patient
        end
    end
end

# Step 2: Create analytical dataset with survival outcomes
analytical_data = DataFrame()

for row in eachrow(eligible_data)
    id = row.patient_id
    time_zero = row.time_zero
    
    # Get follow-up data
    followup_data = filter(r -> r.patient_id == id && 
                          r.visit_date >= time_zero && 
                          r.visit_date <= time_zero + Year(followup_years), 
                          data)
    sort!(followup_data, :visit_date)
    
    # Determine survival outcome
    if any(followup_data.death_indicator .== 1)
        # Death observed
        death_row = followup_data[findfirst(followup_data.death_indicator .== 1), :]
        survival_time = (death_row.visit_date - time_zero).value / 365.25
        event = 1
    else
        # Censored at end of follow-up or last visit
        last_visit = maximum(followup_data.visit_date)
        survival_time = min((last_visit - time_zero).value / 365.25, 
                          followup_years)
        event = 0
    end
    
    analysis_row = DataFrame(
        patient_id = id,
        strategy = row.strategy,
        baseline_cd4 = row.baseline_cd4,
        baseline_age = row.baseline_age,
        survival_time = survival_time,
        event = event
    )
    
    append!(analytical_data, analysis_row)
end

return analytical_data

end

7 Define eligibility criteria

function hiv_eligibility(row) return row.cd4_count >= 350 && row.cd4_count <= 500 && !row.aids_diagnosis && !row.prior_art end

8 Define strategy classifier

function classify_art_strategy(baseline, future_visits) if isempty(future_visits) return “unclassifiable” end

# Strategy 1: Immediate ART (within 30 days)
early_art = any(future_visits.art_initiated .== 1 & 
               (future_visits.visit_date .- baseline.time_zero) .< Day(30))

if early_art
    return "immediate"
end

# Strategy 2: Deferred ART (until CD4 < 350 or AIDS)
# Check if ART was appropriately deferred
art_visits = filter(row -> row.art_initiated == 1, future_visits)

if isempty(art_visits)
    # No ART initiated - appropriate if no indication developed
    developed_indication = any(future_visits.cd4_count .< 350 .| 
                             future_visits.aids_diagnosis .== 1)
    return developed_indication ? "unclassifiable" : "deferred"
else
    # ART initiated - check if appropriately triggered
    first_art = art_visits[1, :]
    pre_art_visits = filter(row -> row.visit_date < first_art.visit_date,
                           future_visits)
    
    if isempty(pre_art_visits)
        return "unclassifiable"
    end
    
    last_pre_art = pre_art_visits[end, :]
    appropriate_trigger = last_pre_art.cd4_count < 350 || 
                        last_pre_art.aids_diagnosis == 1
    
    return appropriate_trigger ? "deferred" : "unclassifiable"
end

end

9 Simulate HIV cohort data for demonstration

Random.seed!(456) n_patients = 500 n_visits_per_patient = 8

hiv_data = DataFrame() for id in 1:n_patients baseline_cd4 = rand(Normal(400, 100)) age = rand(35:65)

for visit in 1:n_visits_per_patient
    visit_date = Date(2010, 1, 1) + Month(6 * (visit - 1))
    
    # CD4 decline over time with noise
    cd4_count = max(50, baseline_cd4 - 20 * (visit - 1) + rand(Normal(0, 30)))
    
    # ART initiation based on guidelines and CD4
    if visit == 1
        art_initiated = 0
        prior_art = false
    else
        prev_art = any(hiv_data[hiv_data.patient_id .== id, :art_initiated] .== 1)
        art_initiated = (cd4_count < 350 || rand() < 0.1) && !prev_art ? 1 : 0
        prior_art = prev_art
    end
    
    # AIDS diagnosis
    aids_diagnosis = cd4_count < 200 && rand() < 0.3
    
    # Death (higher risk with lower CD4)
    death_prob = max(0.001, 0.1 * exp(-(cd4_count / 200)))
    death_indicator = rand() < death_prob ? 1 : 0
    
    visit_row = DataFrame(
        patient_id = id,
        visit_date
